name: claude-hooks-current-gates
tags: gates, gate list, current gates, external gates, gate_rules.yaml, jira hierarchy, git commit gate
body:
Current gates in hooks/gates.py, verified against the actual class list (`grep '^class ' hooks/gates.py`):

Internal (Python classes, need DB access):
- GitCommitGate (Bash): git commit command must contain task:<id> pattern
- GitCommitMcpGate (git__commit): task_id param must be non-empty
- JiraHierarchyGate (tasks__create): story/task must have epic parent; subtask must have bug/story/task parent
- TaskSetActiveGate (tasks__set_active): enforces open/blocked→active via is_valid_transition
- TaskUpdateGate (tasks__update): all status changes go through is_valid_transition
- TaskFinishGate (tasks__finish): enforces →done via is_valid_transition

External (YAML-driven, no Python class per tool — see [[gates-config-driven-external-tools]]):
- Declared in ~/.claude/gate_rules.yaml, loaded by _load_external_gates() into dynamically-built `_ExternalGate_<tool>` instances merged into GATES.
- Current entries (as of this check): imessage__send (prereq contacts__search + name_arg), mail__compose (prereq contacts__search + input_arg), mail__delete (prereq mail__read). Validated end-to-end 2026-07-05.

No manual approval bypass, no review check — review system was removed June 2026 (see [[claude-hooks-review-system]]).

**Correction (2026-07-05):** previous version claimed IMessageSendGate/MailComposeGate/MailDeleteGate as concrete Python Gate subclasses — they were refactored into the YAML-driven external gate mechanism and no longer exist as classes in gates.py. Verified by grepping `^class ` in the file directly, not assumed from an older memory.
